source_jobs.title: Platform Engineering Director
source_jobs.description:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

We are seeking an experienced Platform Engineering Director to manage and lead our Platform Engineering team within the Infrastructure & Operations department. This role reports directly to the VP Infrastructure & Operations and is critical in building and maintaining our cloud infrastructure as well as developer platforms and tooling. The ideal candidate will have a strong technical background, exceptional leadership skills, and a passion for building robust, scalable platform services while ensuring production infrastructure reliability.



What you’ll be doing:

Strategic Leadership

 - Define and execute the platform engineering strategy aligned with business objectives and Infrastructure & Operations goals

 - Lead and manage the Platform Engineering team to high performance

 - Establish and govern best practices, standards, and architecture for platform services and production cloud infrastructure

 - Partner with engineering leadership and Infrastructure & Operations teams to define platform and production system requirements

 - Act as the final escalation point for complex platform engineering issues beyond Level 2 operations support

Platform Development & Infrastructure

 - Oversee the design, development, and maintenance of developer platforms enabling application delivery, CI/CD, and deployment automation

 - Build and maintain platform infrastructure, cloud environments, and tooling in line with architectural standards and requirements

 - Design and operate scalable, highly available, and reliable production systems and services

 - Lead initiatives for infrastructure as code (IaC), observability, and monitoring across development and production environments

 - Provide expert-level technical leadership during complex platform, infrastructure, and production incidents

Team Management

 - Recruit, mentor, and develop platform engineers at all levels

 - Foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement

 - Manage team performance, career development, and succession planning

 - Allocate resources effectively across multiple platform initiatives

 - Build strong cross-functional relationships with Infrastructure & Operations, Engineering, Product, Security, and SRE teams

 - Provide architectural guidance and mentorship to SRE and Level 2 operations teams, including transition-to-support and advanced troubleshooting

Technical Excellence

 - Drive adoption of modern platform engineering practices (e.g. GitOps, service mesh, API gateways)

 - Ensure platform and production infrastructure security, compliance, and cost optimisation

 - Evaluate and integrate new technologies to

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager - B2C
source_jobs.description:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.

The team

We are seeking an Integrated Marketing Manager to serve as the strategic architect for the Ledger Consumer Ecosystem. In this senior role, you will bridge deep product marketing expertise with integrated campaign orchestration, positioning Ledger hardware ("Signers") and the Ledger Wallet app as one unified, secure trading solution.

You are a cross-functional influencer and GTM architect. While dedicated channel owners (CRM, Performance, Social, Web) manage tactical delivery, your mandate is to define the target audience, build the value propositions, and orchestrate the cohesive frameworks and "strategic guardrails" that guide their efforts. You will apply operational rigor to ensure that GTM plans are executed flawlessly across the physical and digital ecosystem.

Your mission

Integrated Strategy & Product Positioning

 - Unified Narrative: Define core positioning, messaging frameworks, and user personas. Champion the "Better Together" narrative that bridges Ledger’s physical hardware and digital services.

 - Strategic Briefing & Guardrails: Translate complex product capabilities into compelling consumer benefits. Develop high-level briefs and strategic guardrails that give creative and channel teams clear direction for impactful assets and campaigns.

 - Thought Leadership: Establish Ledger's overarching market narrative by cultivating strategic relationships with key ecosystem partners, influencers, and industry associations.

 - Collateral Excellence: Create compelling marketing collateral, including website content, product videos, and case studies to effectively communicate value propositions.

2. Go-to-Market (GTM) Orchestration & Operational Rigor

 - GTM Leadership: Own the end-to-end GTM strategy from ideation to launch for new products, features, and major updates.

 - Operational Excellence: Apply operational rigor by managing the flow of creative briefs, tracking launch milestones, and maintaining GTM playbooks to ensure cross-functional teams deliver on time.

 - Cross-Functional Hub: Act as the central hub connecting Product Management, Sales, Engineering, and Regional Marketing to ensure alignment on roadmap priorities.

 - Lifecycle Architecture: Design macro-level blueprints for customer acquisition and engagement. Establish the high-level framework for maximizing long-term Lifetime Value (LTV), guiding CRM and performance teams on targeted retention and "always-on" campaigns.

3. Market Intelligence & Performance Optimization

 - Insights & AI Workflows: Synthesize market research and competitor analysis to inform GTM strategies. Build and maintain AI workflows to track market inte

source_jobs.title: Senior Technical Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



The team:

As a Technical Account Manager for The Americas, based in New York City, you will serve as a linchpin in Ledger Enterprise https://enterprise.ledger.com's global expansion efforts, particularly in the American continent. Reporting directly to the Director of Service Delivery Management, you will be responsible for both pre-sales and post-sales technical support, ensuring that our clients receive an unparalleled service experience.

Your role goes beyond traditional Account Management; you will act as a technical consultant and a strategic partner to our clients, which include digital assets infrastructures like exchanges and custodians, as well as financial institutions and professional investors. You will leverage your deep technical expertise and sales acumen to foster long-term relationships and drive customer satisfaction.




What you'll be doing:

 - Client Onboarding and Support: Manage the onboarding process for key customers, providing them with the necessary technical guidance and documentation. Serve as the primary point of contact for all technical-related queries, ensuring timely and effective resolutions.

 - Operational Monitoring: Keep a close eye on usage patterns and operational activities of key accounts. Use this data to optimize service delivery and preemptively address any potential issues.

 - Technical Leadership: Act as the guarantor for the integration and roll-out of new features and services. Provide the necessary technical guidance and documentation to both internal teams and clients.

 - Incident and Crisis Management: Oversee Technical Support activities and serve as the point of escalation for client service matters. Lead crisis units during major incidents, coordinating between the customer and various technical teams for effective resolution.

 - Performance Metrics and Reporting: Establish and review technical KPIs related to platform performance, such as SLAs and service monitoring. Generate regular reports for internal stakeholders and clients to ensure contract compliance.

 - Sales Support: Provide technical support to pre-sales activities, working closely with the sales team to identify client needs and tailor solutions accordingly.

 - Process Improvement: Actively participate in the improvement of internal processes and best practices, particularly in the management of incidents, problems, and changes.

 - Strategic Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams like Business Development, Marketing, Product, and Research & Development to align technical solutions with business objectives.

 - Client Retention and Growth: Focus on retaining and growing

source_jobs.title: Senior Marketing Manager - Enterprise
source_jobs.description:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.



At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



This role is a high-impact leadership position within the Global Communications and Marketing team, responsible for Ledger’s brand, communication, and strategic narrative for our institutional sector. This role reports directly to the EVP of Communications and Marketing, with a strong dotted line to the EVP of Enterprise Revenue, ensuring total alignment between brand narrative and commercial growth.



We are looking for a powerhouse - someone who isn't just a marketer, but a tenacious industry voice and a social leader. You will play a pivotal role in defining our Enterprise strategy, spearheading high-value institutional adoption, and establishing Ledger as the definitive thought leader in the digital asset space.




YOUR MISSION

 - Champion the Narrative: Partner with the EVP of Communications and Marketing and the EVP of Enterprise Revenue to solidify Ledger Enterprise as the world’s most secure institutional platform and trading tool for critical digital assets and Web3.

 - Strategic Growth & Lead Gen: Architect and execute a global marketing strategy that combines aggressive lead generation with high-value institutional adoption.

 - Industry Thought Leadership: Act as a social and intellectual "face" for the business, proactively shaping industry conversations and building a robust network of institutional influencers.

 - AI-First Execution: Rapidly adapt and integrate AI tooling into the marketing stack to accelerate content creation, data analysis, and workflow efficiency.

 - Build & Inspire: Structure and manage a high-performing, agile team that thrives on cross-functional collaboration and results.

 - Market Intelligence: Stay ahead of the curve on industry trends, regulatory shifts, and emerging technologies, applying these insights to pivot strategies in real-time.

 - Commercial Optimization: Collaborate closely with Sales and Product teams on price testing and competitive research to optimize positioning and maximize revenue.

 - Empathetic Leadership: Serve as a strong, understanding partner to your team and peers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and evolution in a fast-paced environment.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - A Proven Industry Voice: Robust experience in Enterprise product marketing within the financial services industry with a visible track record of social engagement and thought leadership.

 - Tenacious Drive: A "get-it-done" attitude with the grit to navigate the complexities of institutional digital asset landscapes and emerging markets.

 - AI Proficiency: You are an early adopter of AI tools and can demonstrate how you use them to work faster, smarter, and more

source_jobs.title: Affiliate Business Developer - FTC
source_jobs.description:
AFFILIATE BUSINESS DEVELOPER - H/F

Paris, France



We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.



At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



We're seeking a dynamic Affiliate Business Developer for our Consumer Sales team. In this role, you'll drive Ledger's market growth by recruiting affiliates, showcasing our product benefits, and building strong partnerships.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Relationship Management: Foster, grow, and optimize relationships with our top tier global affiliate partners (creators, key opinion leaders, and bloggers) to drive wallet sales and app installs.

 - Active Recruitment: Conduct targeted cold outreach and scale the affiliate network by onboarding partners aligned with Ledger’s self-custody vision.

 - End-to-End Project Management: Coordinate the creation and delivery of dedicated marketing assets (custom landing pages, banners, CRM integration) by collaborating closely with internal Creative, CRM, Tech, and Project Management teams.

 - Collaborative Initiatives: Partner with key internal experts and stakeholders to organize co-branded events, webinars, and partner education programs.

 - Performance & Reporting: Closely monitor, analyze, and optimize affiliate performance metrics. Deliver structured updates and reports to management on affiliate program ROI.

 - Market Analysis: Keep a pulse on market trends and identify new regional or demographic target audiences for strategic affiliate partnerships.




WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - Professional Experience: At least 4+ years of experience in sales, partner acquisition, or account management.

 - Corporate Navigation: A proven track record working within corporate environments. You understand how to navigate internal processes, align multiple departments, and work with cross-functional stakeholders in a larger organization.

 - Project Management Skills: Strong ability to act as a bridge between partners and internal technical/creative teams, ensuring deliverables (like custom web pages or assets) are completed on time.

 - Language Skills: You are a native or fluent English speaker. Since our affiliate scope is entirely global, flawless written and spoken English is non-negotiable.

 - Resilience & Prioritization: Proven capacity to manage stress, multi-task, and ruthlessly prioritize tasks during highly intensive business periods (such as Q4).

 - Web3/Crypto Curiosity: Direct technical experience in Web3 isn't mandatory, but a deep intellectual curiosity and an eagerness to "fall down the crypto rabbit hole" is essential.

 - Availability: Must be ready to start mid-August 2026 to allow for a smooth onboarding hand-off before the maternity leave begins.



At Ledger, we are dedicated to continually investing in our em

source_jobs.title: Staff SecOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



The team:

You’ll join the Security Operations team, responsible for protecting Ledger’s corporate, cloud, SaaS, and data center environments. Its mission: to anticipate, detect, investigate, and respond to cyber threats—including monitoring, alert triage, incident response, detection, visibility, automation, exposure tracking, and continuous process improvement. The scope is distinct from that of the Donjon (product security): SecOps covers the operational security of internal environments, the cloud, endpoints, workloads, identities, and infrastructure.

As a close-knit and experienced team—technically demanding and committed to knowledge sharing—we’re also continuously building the SOC itself: integrating new log sources, ensuring data quality, expanding detection coverage, and developing reliable dashboards and operational workflows.



Our technical stack includes:

 - Splunk for SIEM, investigations, and dashboards;

 - CrowdStrike for EDR and endpoint/workload security;

 - Wiz for cloud security and exposure management;

 - Torq for SOAR and automation;

 - AWS, including modern environments such as EKS/Kubernetes;

 - An in-house developed Agentic SOC for alert enrichment, correlation, investigation support, reporting, and automation.

AI is at the heart of how we work: investing in AI applied to security is a strategic priority for Ledger this year. We’ve built our own in-house Agentic SOC, which autonomously investigates weak signals—the large volume of unreliable alerts that a human team couldn’t sort through manually—and enriches them, so our engineers can focus on what matters most and resolve incidents faster: high-quality detection, noise reduction, and accelerated investigations.



What you’ll be doing:

As a Staff Security Operations Engineer, you're one of the most senior technical voices on the team: a hands-on generalist who can dig deep into any layer of our stack, step back to see the whole picture, and bring clear, calm judgment when the stakes are highest.



Anchor critical-incident response (CSIRT)

 - Bring senior technical leadership to our most complex incidents — cloud, corporate, endpoints, identities, data center — working with the team, not in isolation.

 - Lead complex investigations end to end, alongside the team: root cause analysis, forensics, timeline reconstruction, and remediation that prevents recurrence.

 - Be a trusted escalation point, and keep our handling, escalation, and documentation rigorous and consistent.



Set the direction on detection & threat hunting

 - Set the direction of our detection strategy, architecture, and methodology — with the team — and bring a clear poi

source_jobs.description:

With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



The team

You will join the Security Operations team, which is responsible for protecting Ledger’s corporate, cloud, SaaS, and data center environments. Its mission is to anticipate, detect, investigate, and respond to cyber threats—including monitoring, alert triage, incident response, detection, visibility, automation, exposure tracking, and continuous process improvement. This scope is distinct from that of the Donjon (product security): SecOps covers the operational security of internal environments, the cloud, endpoints, workloads, identities, and infrastructure.

As a close-knit and experienced team—technically rigorous and committed to knowledge sharing—we are also continuously building the SOC itself: integrating new log sources, ensuring data quality, expanding detection coverage, and developing reliable dashboards and operational workflows.

Our technical stack includes:

 - Splunk for SIEM, investigations, and dashboards;

 - CrowdStrike for EDR and endpoint/workload security;

 - Wiz for cloud security and exposure management;

 - Torq for SOAR and automation;

 - AWS, including modern environments such as EKS/Kubernetes;

 - An in-house, internally developed Agentic SOC for alert enrichment, correlation, investigation support, reporting, and automation.

AI is at the heart of how we work: investing in AI applied to security is a strategic priority for Ledger this year. We’ve built our own in-house Agentic SOC, which autonomously investigates weak signals—the large volume of unreliable alerts that a human team couldn’t sort through manually—and enriches them, so our engineers can focus on what matters most and resolve incidents faster: high-quality detection, reduced noise, and accelerated investigations.



What you’ll be doing:

As a Senior Security Operations Engineer, you are at the heart of the SOC: you lead investigations from start to finish, manage the lifecycle of detections, dashboards, and automations, and continuously expand our visibility (cloud, endpoints, identities, SaaS, infrastructure). You work independently on complex issues, decide on the next steps - investigation, containment, remediation, or escalation - serve as a technical resource and point of escalation for more junior analysts (whose work you review and with whom you share your knowledge), and make a tangible contribution to improving our internal Agentic SOC.



Operate the SOC

 - Analyze, classify, and prioritize alerts (from Splunk, CrowdStrike, Wiz, AWS, and other sources), and conduct in-depth investigations into incidents affecting endpoints, the cloud, identities, SaaS, workloads, and infrastructure.

 - Provide clear, actionab

source_jobs.title: Internship - Learning & Development
source_jobs.description:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.



At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



The team: Working closely with the Head of Learning, you will help design and deliver learning experiences that matter — from structured training programs to a company-wide AI adoption initiative involving a network of internal contributors across teams and geographies. You will be the operational backbone of this program: coordinating stakeholders, producing resources, tracking progress, and making sure things actually happen.



What you’ll be doing:

AI Training Program — Coordination & Deployment

 - Onboard and align a network of internal contributors across teams and geographies

 - Plan and facilitate kick-offs, monthly check-ins, and retrospectives

 - Build and maintain shared resources: playbooks, prompt libraries, use-case repositories, and ready to use training modules

 - Turn team AI use cases into concrete learning content

 - Collect feedback and iterate; track adoption metrics and report to leadership

Learning Strategy & Program Support

 - Support the 2026 L&D roadmap rollout across the organization

 - Help managers and VPs scope and deliver their L&D projects

 - Contribute to onboarding follow-ups, KPI tracking, and quarterly impact reports

 - Prepare presentations for ExCom and top management

Content Creation & LMS Administration

 - Design and produce learning materials: videos, e-learning modules, visual guides

 - Collaborate with SMEs to keep content accurate and relevant

 - Administer and optimize our LMS (Sana): user guides, onboarding, usage tracking
   

What we’re looking for:

 - Pursuing a Master's degree in Learning & Development, HR, Communication, Marketing or a related field.

 - Passionate about creating learning experiences and natural coordinator: comfortable managing multiple stakeholders simultaneously and keeping projects on track.

 - Strong written communication skills — able to turn complex topics into clear, engaging content.

 - Proficient / Curious in G-Suite and content creation tools such as Canva or Figma.

 - Curious about AI and comfortable using tools such as Claude, Gemini, or similar in your day-to-day work — hands-on experience is a strong plus.

 - Analytical mindset: at ease with tracking data, drawing insights, and reporting on program impact.

 - Adaptable and resourceful in a fast-paced, multicultural environment — proactive rather than reactive.

 - Fluent in English; French is a plus.

source_jobs.title: E-Commerce Business Line Director
source_jobs.description:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.

 

At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



The mission of the Business Line Director, E-commerce is to lead and grow one of the company's largest revenue and profit streams: the global sales performance of the Ledger e-commerce website across all countries.




THE MISSION:



Key responsibilities include:

 - Strategic Leadership: Defining and executing the global e-commerce strategy, optimizing the user journey, and driving D2C growth for Ledger e-commerce across international markets. What you sell on the website is not just a device or a set of services, it is a full ecosystem that does result in hardware sales but not only.

 - Delivery of Revenue and Growth: Being ultimately accountable for the overall sales performance, conversion rates, and revenue generation of the Ledger e-commerce website globally.

 - Organizational Leadership: Operating with a General Manager mindset, leading the business through extensive cross-functional collaboration. Acting as the central orchestrator between marketing, performance marketing, product, tech, and data teams.

 - Commercial Management: You will directly manage the team in charge of developing Ledger’s affiliate network around the planet. At the frontier between sales-driven business development, relationship management and content creation, this team and our community of affiliates is essential to Ledger growth.

 - Fierce Operational Optimization: Continuously identifying opportunities to optimize the funnel and maximize website performance, leaving no stone unturned when it comes to converting traffic into sales.

 - Market Insight: Maintaining an expert, inside-out understanding of global e-commerce trends, localization strategies, competition, and consumer needs to inform strategic decisions.






WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR :



 - Strong quantitative and strategic commercial acumen: Advancer pricing strategy, Pricing, inventory management, upsell and cross sell of accessories and services, International markets, Shipping and Tax and Duties, none of these have secrets for you.

 - Experience (at least 10 years) with General Manager (GM)-like roles: The ability to understand all facets of the business—strategy, product, go-to-market, and operations—make holistic, P&L-driven decisions and drive execution to over-achievement.

 - Global E-commerce Experience: A track record of success running a large-scale, high-volume global e-commerce and Direct-to-Consumer (D2C) sales business is a must.

 - Delivery and Operational Excellence experience: Proven results in running an e-commerce website with the highest degree of efficiency, optimizing user engagement and conversion rate optimization (CRO).

 - Quantitat

source_jobs.title: Business Line Manager - Swap
source_jobs.description:
About Ledger

We’re a team of experts pushing the limits of what’s possible, united by our common goal to unlock true freedom through digital ownership, making technology accessible for all. We believe in a world where users, creators and enterprises manage their value with ownership and freedom. Our curiosity drives us to innovate, empowering individuals on a global scale. We believe change is constant and our team moves forward as one, with a culture of problem-solving where every employee is empowered and supported to challenge tradition and create solutions. Our mission is simple: to make self-custody accessible and give people the keys to their own financial futures. If you want to make a true impact, we want you to join us at Ledger.



At Ledger, we’re proud to be the global platform for digital assets and Web3, with over 20% of the world’s crypto assets secured through our Ledger devices. With our headquarters in Paris, and offices in Vierzon, Grenoble, Montpellier, London, Portland, Geneva, Zurich and Central Singapore, we have a team of around 600 professionals developing a variety of products and services to enable individuals and companies to securely buy, store, swap, grow and manage crypto assets – including the Ledger hardware wallets line with more than 7.5 millions units already sold in 200 countries.



The mission of the Business Line Manager, Swap is to own and grow the Swap business line end to end: its revenue, its economics, its partner ecosystem, and above all the net rate Ledger delivers to users on every trade. You are the single accountable owner of Swap performance, operating with a Business Manager mindset over a business you run primarily through external providers and cross-functional partners rather than a large direct team.



What you’ll be doing:

 - Business Line Ownership & Revenue Delivery: Full accountability for Swap revenue, take rate, and volume. Building and defending the forecast, understanding every driver of week-over-week movement, and acting on it.

 - Net Delivered Rate as North Star: Owning the competitiveness of the in-wallet quote experience - quote quality and execution slippage combined. Keeping the competitive quote auction healthy and holding partners to their execution commitments.

 - Partner Management & Business Development: Managing, growing, and negotiating with the swap partner bench across CEX-swap, DEX-aggregator, and on-ramp providers - on pricing, coverage, routing eligibility, execution quality, and co-marketing. Managing concentration risk, building substitutability, and onboarding new providers to close coverage and cross-chain gaps.

 - Operational Excellence & Observability: Running the business line with rigour - incident management, error-rate reduction, quote and performance monitoring, and coverage reporting. Protecting the perfect run of existing partners and features is job one.

 - Analytical Leadership: Being the definitive source of truth on Swap. Querying data directly, sizing opportunities and risks quantitatively, and translating analysis into decisions and partner conversations.

 - Cross-Functional Delivery: Driving execution across Product, Technology, Finance, Legal, Pricing, and Marketing to ship improvements and resolve issues outside your direct reporting lines.

 - Market Insight: Maintaining a sharp understanding of the swap and on/off-ramp competitive landscape, provider economics, and cross-chain and DEX-native dynamics to inform positioning and the partner roadmap.
